full_text_rationale: Full text describes SuperLocalMemory as a persistent cross-session local memory system for AI coding agents, including code-graph support, lifecycle hooks, forgetting/consolidation, and retrieval/memory benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
titulo: SuperLocalMemory V3.3: The Living Brain -- Biologically-Inspired Forgetting, Cognitive Quantization, and Multi-Channel Retrieval for Zero-LLM Agent Memory Systems
formato_armazenamento: Local SQLite/sqlite-vec databases including memory.db, learning.db, and code_graph.db; cognitive memory taxonomy; code knowledge graph; lifecycle metadata; Ebbinghaus-style forgetting states; quantized memory tiers; and soft-prompt parameterization artifacts.
framework_agente: SuperLocalMemory V3.3 local-first memory system with MCP server, CLI, dashboard, Claude Code hooks, FRQAD retrieval, seven retrieval channels, adaptive forgetting, and code graph
llm_utilizado: Zero-LLM Mode A evaluation; designed for Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot Chat, Windsurf, MCP-compatible agents, and optional LLM modes
metodo_avaliacao: retrieval benchmark; LoCoMo benchmark; system benchmark; ablation and regression comparison
benchmarks_utilizados: FRQAD retrieval evaluation, LoCoMo Mode A and Mode C memory benchmark, system/package benchmarks
metricas_desempenho: Precision, LoCoMo accuracy, category-level gains, downloads, local CPU/system behavior
metricas_cl: Cross-session memory persistence, lifecycle hooks, forgetting/consolidation, hot/warm/cold/archive quantization, multi-channel retrieval, and code-graph persistence.
baseline_comparado: Cosine retrieval baseline, SuperLocalMemory V3.2 baseline, LoCoMo baselines and Mode C variants
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory is retained locally across sessions in SQLite databases and demoted from Active 32-bit to Warm 8-bit, Cold 4-bit, and Archive 2-bit tiers; the paper does not report a single longitudinal entry-count curve.
custo_por_tarefa: Zero-LLM Mode A runs locally without cloud API calls; no monetary per-task cost is reported.
resultado_principal: FRQAD plus mixed-precision retrieval reports 100% precision versus 85.6% for cosine retrieval. On LoCoMo Mode A, SuperLocalMemory V3.3 reports 70.4% zero-LLM accuracy with larger gains on multi-hop, temporal, and adversarial categories.
desempenho_vs_baseline: The paper reports +23.8 percentage points on multi-hop, +15.3 on temporal, and +12.7 on adversarial questions versus the V3.3 baseline, while V3.3 trails V3.2 overall LoCoMo Mode A by 4.4 percentage points due to fusion complexity.
custo_vs_baseline: Zero-LLM Mode A avoids LLM/API cost and runs on local CPU resources, but the paper does not isolate a comparable monetary-cost baseline.
limitacoes: Fusion complexity hurts simple/single-hop retrieval, fixed channel weights create cold-start sensitivity, and zero-friction lifecycle hooks are Claude Code-specific.
ameacas_validade: Single-system authorship, local benchmark design, LoCoMo mode choices, retrieval-channel weights, platform-specific hooks, and comparison to the prior V3.2 system may limit generalization.
